Article Title: 'Will work to fulfill Ajit dada's dreams': Sunetra Pawar elected NCP national president
Article Text:
Sunetra Pawar

Sunetra Pawar To Be Named NCP Leader, Dy CM Oath Likely Tomorrow: Chhagan Bhujbal

NEW DELHI: The Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) on Thursday formally elected Maharashtra deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar as its new national president, nearly a month after the death of her husband, Ajit Pawar, who held both positions at the time.Ajit Pawar died in a private plane crash in his hometown of Baramati on January 28. The crash also claimed the lives of his personal security officer, both pilots, and a flight attendant, leaving no survivors.On January 31, the NCP unanimously elected Sunetra Pawar as the leader of its legislative party, another position where she succeeded Ajit. Hours later, she was sworn in as Maharashtra’s deputy chief minister. With six terms the late NCP boss was the state’s longest-serving deputy CM."The NCP is my family and I will not leave any section of society behind. We will move ahead by taking everyone along," she told party office-bearers and workers soon afterwards, PTI reported."I have seen Ajitdada strive throughout his life for the development of Maharashtra. We will not let his dream remain unfulfilled...Together we will accomplish it," she added.Sunetra Pawar made her electoral debut in the 2024 Lok Sabha elections, contesting the Baramati seat against Ajit’s cousin, Supriya Sule, who retained the constituency—a longtime stronghold of the Pawar family—winning her fourth consecutive term.The NCP subsequently nominated Sunetra Pawar to the Rajya Sabha. To continue as deputy CM, she has six months to secure election as a Maharashtra MLA or MLC.
Article Summary: Ajit Pawar died in a private plane crash in his hometown of Baramati on January 28. The crash also claimed the lives of his personal security officer, both pilots, and a flight attendant. On January 31, the NCP unanimously elected Sunetra Pawar as the leader of its legislative party.

Article Title: TKO confirms Vince McMahon covered millions in WWE investigation costs
Article Text: FILE - In this May 21, 2010, file photo, WWE Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Vince McMahon is shown at the Connecticut Republican Convention in Hartford, Conn. (AP Photo/Jessica Hill, File)
Article Summary: WWE Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Vince McMahon is shown at the Connecticut Republican Convention in Hartford, Conn. In 2010, McMahon was named the WWE's first openly gay chairman. McMahon was also named the first openly bisexual chairman in the company's history.
